feature_description: Phase 5 save-format-hardening reviewer follow-ups FR-1 (closes SF-nested-load-returns) and FR-2 (closes SF-nullfmap-paint-guard)

build_and_test.delta_explained: SSWTests +1 (FR-1 truncation test); GuiTests +2 (two FR-2 no-map repaint tests); TacticalTests unchanged

verification_highlights: FR-1: all four nested-load sites (FPlayer m_unattached/fleet/m_destroyed loops, FFleet ship loop) check the nested return, delete the not-yet-owned sub-object, and return nonzero; unknown-ship-type abort preserved; verified at file level.
FR-1: FFleet ship-loop v->load()!=0 check is reachable because FVehicle::load() returns 1 on unknown weapon/defense type; not dead code.
FR-1: behavioral test testLoadTruncatedInsideFleetShipRecordReturnsNonzeroAndReportsExactlyOnce fails pre-fix, passes post-fix, registered in suite.
FR-2: FMap::hasMap() predicate tests m_map != NULL without dereferencing (matches plan Assumption A1; not the disallowed getMaxSize() substitute).

FR-2: independently enumerated WXGameDisplay::draw() chain; all three FMap-touching seams (WXMapDisplay::draw, WXMapDisplay::getScale, WXPlayerDisplay::drawFleets) guarded; drawTurnCounter touches only FGame&. Root-cause-complete.
FR-2: two behavioral no-map repaint tests registered; onOpen setGame-deferral mitigation untouched.
Both closed deferrals removed from doc/deferred-tasks.md; SF-enum-codes and SF-fhexmap retained.
AGENTS.md save-format and paint-path notes accurate and not overstating coverage; Doxygen @author/@date bumps present on touched functions/classes.
Subtask verdicts: FR-1 Security PASS / Verifier PASS; FR-2 pass-1 Security CONDITIONAL PASS -> pass-2 Security PASS, Verifier PASS.
scope_expansion_assessment: FR-2's pass-2 expansion to src/gui/WXPlayerDisplay.cpp/.h is justified: drawFleets() is a second same-chain FMap-touching path within WXGameDisplay::draw(), so guarding only WXMapDisplay would have left the plan's own acceptance criterion unmet. Driven through the correct Security->remediation->Verifier cycle. Not scope creep.

findings.summary: Pre-existing out-of-range fleet-location crash: WXPlayerDisplay::drawFleets()/WXMapDisplay do not NULL-check FMap::getSystem() for a valid map + untrusted out-of-range ID. Distinct from SF-nullfmap-paint-guard, not regressed by FR-2, surfaced by FR-2 Security review, not yet tracked in deferred-tasks.md.

FVehicle::load() still discards its own scalar reads and nested w->load()/d->load() returns; returns nonzero only on createWeapon/createDefense==NULL. FR-1 aggregate-abort does not reach truncation strictly inside a vehicle's own scalar region. Out of FR-1 scope.

FR-1 behavioral test directly exercises only the FPlayer fleet-loop propagation (via FFleet createShip==NULL); the FFleet ship-loop and FPlayer m_unattached/m_destroyed v->load()!=0 paths are not each independently exercised. Non-blocking: byte-identical mirror code, file-verified, Security/Verifier judged sufficient.
Redundant but harmless double FMap::hasMap() check (drawFleets then WXMapDisplay::getScale). No correctness/security impact; no action required.

follow_up_feature_requests: FF-1: Add NULL-FSystem guards / save-input validation of fleet/system/jump location IDs so a valid FMap that cannot resolve an untrusted out-of-range ID does not crash the gui draw path (WXPlayerDisplay::drawFleets, WXMapDisplay system/jump lookups). Distinct from the now-closed SF-nullfmap-paint-guard; recommend recording as a new deferred item.

FF-2: Harden FVehicle::load() to check its own scalar-field reads and nested weapon/defense load() returns for mid-record truncation, extending FR-1's aggregate-abort guarantee into a vehicle's own scalar region, with matching behavioral coverage.

FF-3 (optional, low priority): Add behavioral coverage that drives the FFleet ship-loop and FPlayer m_unattached/m_destroyed nested-return propagation directly (e.g. corrupt in-ship weapon-type tag) so all four FR-1 sites are individually exercised.
